calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Peter Hwu, Esq., 415.398.8329, for Plaintiff/Judgment Creditor Gao's Group Inc.;
Atty. William J. Campbell, Esq. of Fitzgerald & Campbell, 855.709.5788, for Defendant/Judgment Debtor Shawn Nichols.

PLAINTIFF GAO'S GROUP,INC.'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

Matter called, DISCUSSIONS are held.

The Court informs Mr. Hwu that the judgment creditor may not take a claim of exemption off calendar. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Judgment debtor Shawn Nichols' claim of exemption is granted in the amount of $6,827.00.

Judgment debtor claims an exemption under Code of Civil Procedure section 704.120 and Exec. Order N-57-20 on the ground that the sources of funds levied upon in the amount of $6,827.00 constituted exempt unemployment benefits and stimulus payments. Judgment debtor shows under penalty of perjury that the sole source of the funds in the account were transfers from an EDD debit card ($16,690) and a COVID-19 stimulus check ($2,400). Judgment debtor meets his burden of tracing exempt funds. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 703.080(b).)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment Or Vacate Renewal Of Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-18-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Peter Hwu, Esq., 415.398.8329, for Plaintiff Gao's Group Inc.;
Atty. William J. Campbell, Esq. of Fitzgerald & Campbell, 855.709.5788, for Defendant Shawn Nichols.

DEFENDANT SHAWN NICHOLS' Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment Or Vacate Renewal Of Judgment.

Matter called, ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant's motion to vacate the renewal of the judgment or in the alternative, to set aside the default and default judgment is denied.

(See Word minutes this date for the Court's complete ruling.)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Matter is continued on the Court's own motion to March 16, 2021 to be heard together with the motion to set aside default judgment or vacate renewal of judgment.
